title: The Lava Ferry
desc: Supplies need crossing. The ferry is a stone raft on molten rock.

steps.text:
The lava channel between the supply depot and the residential warrens is forty feet across. It's spanned by a ferry — a flat slab of heat-resistant stone on iron chains, pulled across by a hand-crank mechanism that looks older than the UnderWorld itself.

The ferry operator is a squat creature who's been doing this job so long the heat has permanently reddened her skin. She nods at three crates of supplies on the dock.

"Load. Crank. Unload. Don't fall in. I'm taking a break." She sits on a rock and produces a sandwich.

The lava below bubbles gently. It's beautiful, in the way that things that will kill you instantly are sometimes beautiful.

options.label: Load the crates and crank across

steps.text:
You load the crates — grain, iron stock, and something labeled FRAGILE DO NOT DROP OR EVERYONE DIES. You choose not to investigate.

The crank is stiff. Each rotation pulls the stone raft forward about a foot. The lava licks at the edges of the platform, leaving orange-black crusts where it touches. Heat radiates upward in waves that blur the far dock into a shimmer.

Halfway across, the crank catches. The chain has sagged into the lava. You can see it glowing red where it dips below the surface. You crank harder. The chain groans, stretches, lifts clear of the lava trailing fire, and catches again.

You make it to the far dock with singed eyebrows and a crank arm that's too hot to touch. The receiving crew unloads the crates. Nobody mentions the FRAGILE one. You don't either.

options.label: Crank back to the other side — without cargo

steps.text:
Smart move. You grab the chain and pull it taut. Three links in, you find the problem — a link that's been heat-stressed, the metal crystallized and brittle. It hasn't broken yet, but it's close.

The operator looks up from her sandwich. "Yeah. That link. I've been meaning to flag it." She hasn't been meaning to flag it.

You find a replacement link in the maintenance bin, swap it out with pliers that are warm from proximity to the lava, and test the crank. Smooth. The raft glides to the center and back without a hitch.

The crates go across without incident. No glowing chains, no near-misses, no existential dread.

The operator finishes her sandwich. "Efficient. Come back tomorrow — I need someone to oil the pulleys." She pauses. "Actually, I might take another break. You can do the pulleys yourself."

options.label: Consider a career in lava logistics
